rulings.Case Title: SVIATLANA NAHORNAYA VS. ANNA NADELSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 1, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT SVIATLANA NAHORNAYA, VOLHA NAHORNAYA MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is moot. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SVIATLANA NAHORNAYA VS. ANNA NADELSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 1, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT SVIATLANA NAHORNAYA, VOLHA NAHORNAYA DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action (including but not limited to damages re: breach of contract; terms of the contract if the alleged contract is oral (as suggested in opposition) and mutual understanding of the parties as to the term "short-term"; actionable misrepresentation; reliance on such misrepresentation and damages re: negligent misrepresentation; outrageous conduct re: IIED). Plaintiff should be mindful of sham pleadings doctrine and the express language of the 12/13/2018 contract. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SHARON SCHWARTZ VS. KEN FRIEDERS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 1, 2021 Line 3. Demurrer To Complaint is SUSTAIEND without leave to amend as to the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. (Opposition 4:5-6). Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to causes of action for breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing and Elder Abuse for Plaintiff to allege facts giving rise to these causes of action based on "subsequent and continuing acts" (Opposition 7:23) not arising out of the refusal to pay on insurance claim (addressed in Small Claims action). Lincoln Property Co., N.C., Inc. v. Travelers Indemnity Co. (2006) 137 Cal.App.4th 905. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CONARD HOUSE, INC. VS. THOMAS MITCHELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 1, 2021 Line 7. DEFENDANT THOMAS MITCHELL MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is continued to October 10, 2021. By no later than October 8, 2021 (1) defendant to submit a declaration from a medical professional indicating that Defendant's condition prevented him/contributed to his inability to timely inform his counsel or his social worker regarding service of summons and complaint; whether defendant is able to assist in his own defense going forward, e.g. verify discovery responses, testify at trial; and or can enter into binding agreements (e.g. behavioral stipulation suggested by defendant's counsel) and (2) defendant to submit a supplemental brief analyzing authorities, both cases and legislative history, standing for the proposition that Plaintiff's counsel may be the "offending" attorney under CCP 473(c), when relief is requested and/or granted under CCP 473(b) on discretionary and/or mandatory grounds. Supplemental brief not to exceed 10 pages. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
